title: rootless: fix exec
body:
We cannot re-exec into a new user namespace to gain privileges and
access an existing as the new namespace is not the owner of the
existing container.

"unshare" is used to join the user namespace of the target container.

The current implementation assumes that the main process of the
container didn't create a new user namespace.

Since in the setup phase we are not running with euid=0, we must skip
the setup for containers/storage.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1329
Closes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1337

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body: I've left the WIP tag as I am not really happy of the new code and I'd like to spend some more time on this issue to see if it can be addressed in a nicer way
I restarted the Jenkins Job that hit the timeout. I see quite some timeouts for a few days. Maybe we have to put our CI and the tests on another diet.
@giuseppe You feel comfortable getting this in today's release, or would you like more time to work on it?
@mheon I've looked more but I don't see any other nice way of solving it :(
@giuseppe Alright, fair enough. Let's review and merge for release, then
We should go through and find all the uses of the store, and add nil checks to make sure we can produce reasonable errors if somehow skip storage gets set for another command
@rhatdan @vrothberg @baude PTAL
@giuseppe Cross-build errors in Travis
LGTM
integrations tests are failing now. Taking a look..
Rootless tests seem to be failing on Travis
hm.. they pass locally, can't understand what is going wrong on Travis
Travis uses some ancient kernel that lacks the ioctl that is needed to lookup the parent of an user namespace.  I've added a check for that and now tests are happy.
